mcqs.question: What is the average-case time complexity of Randomized Quicksort?
mcqs.explanation: Randomized Quicksort has an average-case time complexity of O(n log n) due to random pivot selection ensuring balanced partitions on average.

mcqs.question: What is the worst-case time complexity of Randomized Quicksort?
mcqs.explanation: The worst-case remains O(n²), though the probability of this happening is extremely low (1/n! for sorted input).

mcqs.question: Why does standard Quicksort perform poorly on sorted arrays?
mcqs.explanation: When picking first/last element as pivot on sorted array, partitions become highly unbalanced (n-1 and 0 elements), leading to O(n²).

mcqs.question: What is the main purpose of randomization in Randomized Quicksort?
mcqs.explanation: Randomization eliminates dependency on input distribution, protecting against adversarial inputs that cause worst-case behavior in standard Quicksort.

mcqs.question: What is the expected number of comparisons in Randomized Quicksort for n elements?
mcqs.explanation: The expected comparisons is approximately 2n ln n ≈ 1.39n log n, proven using indicator random variables in probability analysis.

mcqs.question: Which technique is used to analyze the expected running time of Randomized Quicksort?

options.text: Indicator random variables and linearity of expectation

mcqs.explanation: The analysis uses indicator random variables where each pair of elements has probability 1/2 of being compared, applying linearity of expectation.

mcqs.question: What is the space complexity of Randomized Quicksort?
mcqs.explanation: O(log n) auxiliary space is needed for the recursion stack in the average case. Worst-case space is O(n).

mcqs.question: Is Randomized Quicksort a stable sorting algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: Randomized Quicksort is not stable because during partitioning, elements with equal keys may be swapped to different positions.

mcqs.question: What is the probability that a particular element becomes the pivot in Randomized Quicksort?
mcqs.explanation: Since the pivot is chosen uniformly at random from n elements, each element has probability 1/n of being selected as pivot.

mcqs.question: What improvement does three-way partitioning add to Randomized Quicksort?

options.text: Handles duplicates efficiently by creating three partitions

mcqs.explanation: Three-way partitioning (Dutch National Flag) divides array into < pivot, = pivot, > pivot, handling duplicate values efficiently.

mcqs.question: Which of the following is NOT true about Randomized Quicksort?

options.text: Expected time is O(n log n)

mcqs.explanation: Randomized Quicksort can have O(n²) worst-case, but it's not guaranteed. The probability of worst-case is extremely low.

mcqs.question: In Randomized Quicksort, what is the probability that the smallest and largest elements are compared?
mcqs.explanation: Any two elements are compared with probability 1/2 because one becomes pivot before the other is separated into different partitions.

mcqs.question: What is tail recursion optimization in Randomized Quicksort?

options.text: Converts second recursive call to iteration to reduce stack space

mcqs.explanation: Tail recursion optimization converts the second recursive call into iteration, reducing worst-case stack space from O(n) to O(log n).

mcqs.question: Introsort combines Quicksort with which algorithm?
mcqs.explanation: Introsort starts with Quicksort and switches to Heapsort when recursion depth exceeds a threshold, guaranteeing O(n log n) worst-case.

mcqs.question: How many partitions are created in the standard partition function of Randomized Quicksort?
mcqs.explanation: The standard partition creates two partitions: elements less than pivot and elements greater than pivot.

mcqs.question: What is the key advantage of randomized pivot selection?

options.text: Faster than all other sorting algorithms
Independent of input distribution, no worst-case input exists

mcqs.explanation: Randomization makes the algorithm's performance independent of input arrangement, eliminating the possibility of adversarial worst-case inputs.

mcqs.question: Which sorting algorithm is Randomized Quicksort similar to in terms of divide-and-conquer?
mcqs.explanation: Both Randomized Quicksort and Merge Sort use divide-and-conquer: divide the problem, conquer recursively, and combine results.

mcqs.question: What happens when all elements in the array are equal in Randomized Quicksort?
mcqs.explanation: With equal elements and standard partition (choosing last element as pivot), one partition gets n-1 elements and other gets 0, causing O(n²).

mcqs.question: The recurrence T(n) = T(n-1) + O(n) represents which case of Randomized Quicksort?
mcqs.explanation: When one partition is empty (n-1, 0), we get T(n) = T(n-1) + O(n), which solves to O(n²), representing the worst case.

mcqs.question: What is the probability of O(n²) worst-case occurring in Randomized Quicksort for n distinct elements?
mcqs.explanation: For the worst-case (always getting min or max as pivot), all n pivots must be chosen in sorted order, probability 1/n!
